dimensions.note: Change is a pure derivation (classifyStackPresence + rollups) over already-fetched StackInstanceSummary fields; introduces no new credential paths, env reads, or vault references. AWS auth still resolves via the existing environment/profile credential chain.
No logging calls added or changed. The existing structured entry/completion info log on runAudit is untouched; the hint and rollups emit nothing and log no sensitive data.

classifyStackPresence is a total function (every path returns; no throw); the orphanCandidates/byStackPresenceHint computation is pure array filtering after instances are built, so it adds no new failure path and no broad try/catch. Data is still written only after computation succeeds.

Mutation-verified teeth. Truth-table unit tests cover all 5 contract branches incl. the rule-1-wins edge (SUCCEEDED with empty stackId => likely-absent). Flipping SKIPPED_SUSPENDED_ACCOUNT->unknown turned the rule-3 unit test AND the smoke rollup RED. Dropping the SUCCEEDED guard on the actionable filter turned the smoke actionable=1 assertion RED. Re-pointing byStackPresenceHint to failureCategory turned the present=6/likely-absent=211 assertions RED. Removing the build-site stackPresenceHint assignment is a COMPILE error (TS2322: required field of InstanceRecord), so a forgotten fresh-write cannot be masked by the schema .default. Full suite: 42 passed / 0 failed.
Read-only audit model; the single audit method mutates no cloud state. The added hint/rollups are derived output only, so create/delete idempotency does not apply.

No new AWS API calls or response-shape assumptions. The hint derives solely from (detailedStatus, stackId) already pulled from StackInstanceStatus.DetailedStatus and StackId, both already nullish-coalesced to '' at the build site.
No new file handles, network clients, or temp files. Pure in-memory derivation over the existing instances array.

Verified clean. The new schema .describe() blocks and JSDoc now direct operators to confirm with '@jentz/aws-cfn-orphan-sweep enumerate (run against an org-scoped model for org-wide coverage)' — an accurate reference: the sibling extension exposes enumerate (read-only) and cleanup, and org-wide coverage is a model-config/profile concern (its README shows org-wide use as plain enumerate). The earlier dangling 'enumerateOrg' reference is gone (rg -n enumerateOrg --hidden is empty repo-wide). Synthetic-identifier hygiene clean: new smoke fixtures generate accounts via padStart(12,'0') sequential ids (000000000001+) and SID arns from those; no real account ids/arns introduced. Manifest version, model.version, and final upgrades.toVersion are aligned at 2026.06.23.1.

Mechanical schema-write conformance verified. New fields use strict shapes: stackPresenceHint is z.enum(['present','likely-absent','unknown']); byStackPresenceHint is z.record(string,number); orphanCandidates is a closed z.object({actionable,inaccessible}). No .passthrough(). Every schema field is written at the build site: the instances array is typed InstanceRecord[] (so omitting stackPresenceHint is a compile error), and the summary object sets byStackPresenceHint and orphanCandidates. No data field is absent from a schema; no placeholder values.
Resource specs (summary, instance) retain lifetime: 'infinite' and garbageCollection: 10; the change adds no spec and alters no lifetime/GC setting.
Intentionally read-only audit model with a single fan-out audit method; no create/update/delete/sync lifecycle is in scope. The colocated metadata test asserts methods === ['audit'].
No mutating method exists, so preflight check labels (policy/live) do not apply. The audit method makes only read-only AWS calls.

Mechanical instance-name review: instance rows are still keyed by instanceKey(account, region) and the summary by the fixed 'summary' name; the change adds no writeResource call and no new spec, so naming uniqueness across the audit execution is unchanged. The new smoke fixture uses distinct sequential accounts so its 217 instance rows do not collide.

No readResource or dataRepository access added or changed. The hint and rollups are computed from the in-memory instances array during the same audit run, consistent with the repository rule to reference already-materialized data rather than re-fetch.

Version bumped 2026.06.22.0 -> 2026.06.23.1 in manifest.yaml and model.version, with a matching final upgrades entry (toVersion 2026.06.23.1, pass-through upgradeAttributes). Invariant test asserts manifest==model.version==upgrades.at(-1).toVersion. Old-data-load verified by parsing a 2026.06.22.0-shaped instance (no stackPresenceHint) and an old summary (no new fields) through the field definitions: stackPresenceHint defaults to 'unknown', byStackPresenceHint to {}, orphanCandidates to {actionable:0,inaccessible:0}, so the pass-through upgrade loads pre-existing persisted resources without error.
